title: LIFTING POINTS AND TOW TRUCK TOWING

figures_detected.description: Two illustrations showing NISSAN recommended towing method with driving (front) wheels off the ground — both flatbed and wheel-lift tow truck configurations shown
Illustration showing incorrect towing of N-CVT model with rear wheels raised and front wheels on ground, with shading indicating prohibited method

content_blocks.text: All applicable local laws regarding the towing operation must be obeyed.
It is necessary to use proper towing equipment to avoid possible damage to the vehicle during a towing operation.
When towing with the rear wheels on the ground, release the parking brake and move the gearshift lever to neutral ("N" position).
NISSAN recommends that vehicles be towed with the driving (front) wheels off the ground as illustrated.
TOWING AN N-CVT MODEL WITH FOUR WHEELS ON GROUND
Before towing an N-CVT model with four wheels on ground, the N-CVT fluid level should be checked.
Observe the following restricted towing speeds and distances.

items.label: Speed
items.condition: N-CVT model, four wheels on ground

items.label: Distance
items.condition: N-CVT model, four wheels on ground

content_blocks.text: Never tow an N-CVT model from the rear (i.e., backward) with four wheels on the ground as this may cause serious and expensive damage to the transaxle.
TOWING AN N-CVT MODEL WITH REAR WHEELS RAISED (With front wheels on ground)

Never tow an N-CVT model with rear wheels raised (with front wheels on ground) as this may cause serious and expensive damage to the transaxle. If it is necessary to tow it with rear wheels raised, always use a towing dolly under the front wheels.

model_notes: N-CVT model specific towing restrictions apply. M/T models may be towed with rear wheels on ground provided parking brake is released and gearshift is in neutral.
